content:
# Research: codebase-to-course Product Evaluation for Corvia (March 2026)

## Product

codebase-to-course by zarazhangrui (github.com/zarazhangrui/codebase-to-course). A Claude Code skill that turns any codebase into a single-page interactive HTML course for non-technical "vibe coders."

## How it works

Four-phase pipeline:
1. Deep codebase read: identify actors (components/services), trace user journeys end-to-end, map data flows, discover engineering patterns
2. Curriculum ordering: concrete to abstract. Starts from what the user knows (app behavior) and zooms into code
3. Module generation: 4-8 modules, each teaching one practical skill (steering AI, debugging, decision-making)
4. HTML rendering: single self-contained file with animations, code translations, quizzes, glossary tooltips

## Key analysis techniques (applicable to corvia)

### 1. Top-down user-journey tracing
Starts from user-facing entry points (routes, CLI commands, event handlers) and traces data flow outward. Discovers architecture by following "what happens when you click X." This is the inverse of dependency-graph traversal.

### 2. Actor/character semantic grouping
Assigns each component a "character" with a role in the system. Not just structural grouping (directory or imports) but semantic: "what is this component's responsibility?" Maps files to actors, then teaches actor interactions.

### 3. Cross-cutting pattern discovery
Identifies recurring patterns across the whole codebase: caching strategies, error handling, validation, middleware chains. Also identifies inconsistencies between modules. These are cross-cutting concerns that per-module analysis misses entirely.

### 4. Concrete-to-abstract ordering
Module progression: user experience first, then components, then communication patterns, then external dependencies, then engineering patterns, then debugging, then full architecture overview. The ordering principle is "what helps the learner steer AI better?" which translates to "what helps agents understand the system?"

## What doesn't apply to corvia

- HTML output format (corvia writes knowledge graph entries, not documents)
- Animation, quiz, tooltip generation (pedagogical tools for human learners)
- Metaphor generation (for non-technical audience)
- "Vibe coder" framing (corvia serves AI agents, not beginners)
- Single-file constraint (corvia has a proper knowledge store)

## Impact on corvia's deep ingest design

Three gaps identified and addressed:
1. Original design was bottom-up only. Added top-down entry-point tracing for system narratives.
2. Original design used directory/graph grouping only. Added LLM semantic actor identification.
3. Original design was per-module only. Added cross-cutting pattern scan pass.
